home.heroTitle: Warehouse staffing outsourcing — Moscow & Moscow Oblast
home.heroSubtitle: Shift-based SLA: attendance, replacements, transparent hourly rates, compliance. We do not provide outstaffing.
home.trustLead: Operations teams at marketplaces, logistics and manufacturing — one line-staff standard.

homePage.trustSrOnly: Sectors where we supply staff; icons are illustrative.

personas.title: Three stakeholders, one solution
personas.lead: Warehouse staffing decisions involve different risks for ops, HR and finance — we close each loop separately and as a system.

cards.title: Warehouse director / COO
cards.body: You need attendance, replacements and a single accountable party per shift — without HR noise in daily ops.

cards.title: CHRO / HRD
cards.body: Less load on internal recruiting in peaks: clear profiles, time-to-floor and contract-grade reporting.

cards.title: CFO
cards.body: Predictable cost per shift: hourly benchmarks and peak scenarios so the model matches reality.

pain.title: Problem → solution
pain.lead: The bento grid highlights priorities: labour shortage and compliance are the “heavy” tiles; speed and outcome tie to KPIs.

deficit.title: Labour shortage
deficit.body: Seasonal peaks, line churn and shift gaps without a predictable pool and onboarding.

compliance.title: Compliance
compliance.body: Document trail without quietly pushing risk to the client.

speed.title: Speed
speed.body: Time-to-shift and reporting for COO/HRD in one operating loop.

outcome.title: Contractor as operating system
outcome.body: Shift coverage, swaps, attendance control and transparent KPIs — from pilot one.

stats.kicker: Numbers a COO can take to a steering meeting

fullBleed.title: What the floor and the report both show
fullBleed.body: Shifts, replacements, migration compliance and attendance — one data plane. The background layer adds depth; on-site shoots can swap in photo or video without layout changes.
bullets: Single SLA for shift start and replacement escalation
Clear document trail for audits and inspections
COO reporting: attendance, incidents, cost per shift

techItems.d: Checklists, notification deadlines, controlled document versions.
Lead qualification and draft articles for human edit.
Attendance, swaps, shift incidents — one reporting surface.

sections.reviewsLead: Initials and roles; with publication approval — full names and logos.
sections.ctaLead: Multi-step form: contacts, project parameters, personal data consent.

faq.a: That’s the model: the contractor runs shifts and on-site attendance, not outstaffing. For DCs and 3PL it cuts procurement noise.

faq.q: How fast can we ramp volume?
faq.a: Pilot — from a few days; scale depends on profiles and location. Details go into the commercial proposal.
In the standard contractor model the document loop sits with us; legal finalises contract wording.

faq.q: Do you cover the whole Moscow Oblast?
faq.a: Yes — programmatic coverage for 30 MO cities plus Moscow.
SLA is agreed case by case and written into the contract annex.

faq.q: How do we get a proposal?
faq.a: Via the request form or calculator — data lands in CRM and analytics events.

faq.q: Do you work nights and weekends?
faq.a: Yes. Shift patterns are agreed in the proposal: nights, weekends and peaks with separate coefficients in the model.
The contract defines replacement procedure and response window; actual SLAs are tuned per site and profile.
Typically 2–4 weeks — enough to validate attendance, onboarding quality and shift reporting.

faq.q: Can we start with part of the site?
faq.a: Yes — we begin with a zone or line and expand after metrics stabilise, without a “big bang” day one.

reviews.name: Alexey K.
reviews.quote: Attendance and replacements became predictable after the first pilot month — finally one accountable loop.

reviews.name: Marina S.
reviews.quote: Migration compliance stopped being a black box for internal audit — statuses and deadlines are visible in the report.

reviews.name: Dmitry V.

reviews.name: Elena P.
reviews.quote: Shift reporting finally matched the peak-season financial model — finance and ops speak the same language.

reviews.name: Igor N.
reviews.quote: Crew onboarding stays within the promised SLA; night replacements don’t turn into chaos.

reviews.name: Olga T.
reviews.quote: Documents and notification deadlines under control — no surprises on external inspections.
